title: Park Visitors by Region
description: You are given three tables: regions, parks in each region, and visit records. Find distinct visitor names who have visited at least one park in the 'California' region, ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
regions(id, name, country)
parks(id, name, region_id)
visits(id, park_id, visitor_name)
outputFormat: One visitor name per line, ordered alphabetically. No duplicates.
constraints: Use two JOINs and DISTINCT. Filter by region name. Include ORDER BY visitor_name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT DISTINCT v.visitor_name FROM visits v JOIN parks p ON v.park_id = p.id JOIN regions r ON p.region_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'California' ORDER BY v.visitor_name;
hint: Join visits → parks → regions, filter WHERE region name = 'California', then use DISTINCT.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE regions (id INTEGER, name TEXT, country TEXT); CREATE TABLE parks (id INTEGER, name TEXT, region_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE visits (id INTEGER, park_id INTEGER, visitor_name TEXT); INSERT INTO regions VALUES (1,'California','US'),(2,'Texas','US'),(3,'Florida','US'); INSERT INTO parks VALUES (1,'Yosemite',1),(2,'Big Sur',1),(3,'Zion',2),(4,'Everglades',3); INSERT INTO visits VALUES (1,1,'Alice'),(2,1,'Bob'),(3,2,'Alice'),(4,3,'Charlie'),(5,4,'Diana');

CREATE TABLE regions (id INTEGER, name TEXT, country TEXT); CREATE TABLE parks (id INTEGER, name TEXT, region_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE visits (id INTEGER, park_id INTEGER, visitor_name TEXT); INSERT INTO regions VALUES (1,'California','US'),(2,'Oregon','US'),(3,'Nevada','US'); INSERT INTO parks VALUES (1,'Muir Woods',1),(2,'Crater Lake',2),(3,'Valley of Fire',3); INSERT INTO visits VALUES (1,1,'Eve'),(2,2,'Frank'),(3,1,'Grace'),(4,3,'Hank'),(5,1,'Frank');

CREATE TABLE regions (id INTEGER, name TEXT, country TEXT); CREATE TABLE parks (id INTEGER, name TEXT, region_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE visits (id INTEGER, park_id INTEGER, visitor_name TEXT); INSERT INTO regions VALUES (1,'California','US'),(2,'Arizona','US'); INSERT INTO parks VALUES (1,'Joshua Tree',1),(2,'Grand Canyon',2); INSERT INTO visits VALUES (1,2,'Iris'),(2,2,'Jack');

CREATE TABLE regions (id INTEGER, name TEXT, country TEXT); CREATE TABLE parks (id INTEGER, name TEXT, region_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE visits (id INTEGER, park_id INTEGER, visitor_name TEXT); INSERT INTO regions VALUES (1,'California','US'),(2,'Utah','US'); INSERT INTO parks VALUES (1,'Sequoia',1),(2,'Arches',2); INSERT INTO visits VALUES (1,1,'Kate'),(2,1,'Leo'),(3,1,'Mia'),(4,2,'Nan');

CREATE TABLE regions (id INTEGER, name TEXT, country TEXT); CREATE TABLE parks (id INTEGER, name TEXT, region_id INTEGER); CREATE TABLE visits (id INTEGER, park_id INTEGER, visitor_name TEXT); INSERT INTO regions VALUES (1,'California','US'),(2,'Washington','US'); INSERT INTO parks VALUES (1,'Point Reyes',1),(2,'Olympic',2); INSERT INTO visits VALUES (1,1,'Owen'),(2,1,'Owen'),(3,2,'Owen');
